2606.31991.title: Amplifying Membership Signal Through Chained Regeneration
2606.31991.paper_summary: The tendency of large generative models to memorize training data makes sample verification critical for privacy auditing and copyright enforcement. Current membership (MIA) and dataset inference (DI) attacks often rely on one-shot generations, which yield weak signals and limited sensitivity across modalities. Inspired by Model Autophagy Disorder (MAD), we introduce MADreMIA, a model-agnostic framework that enhances white-, gray-, and black-box MIA and DI. Rather than relying on shadow model training -- often infeasible for large generative models -- our framework facilitates scalable inference by leveraging inherent signals through iterative trajectories. This process utilizes chained generations across diverse modalities, where each output serves as the subsequent input, to improve membership evidence at low FPR. We demonstrate that memorized training samples exhibit significantly higher coherence and slower degradation during iterative regeneration than non-member generations. Our results show that MADreMIA provides richer signals across diverse model families and modalities; we present comprehensive evaluations for IARs, diffusion, and language models, alongside preliminary results demonstrating its potential for audio models.
2606.31991.paper_summary_zh: 大型生成模型記憶訓練數據的趨勢使得樣本驗證對於隱私審計和版權執行至關重要。當前的成員身份攻擊（MIA）和數據集推斷攻擊（DI）通常依賴於一次性生成，這會產生微弱的信號和有限的跨模態敏感性。受到模型自噬障礙（MAD）的啟發，我們提出了MADreMIA，一個與模型無關的框架，增強了白盒、灰盒和黑盒的MIA和DI。我們的框架不依賴於影子模型訓練——這對於大型生成模型而言通常不可行——而是通過利用內在信號來促進可擴展的推斷，通過迭代軌跡進行。這一過程利用跨多種模態的鏈式生成，其中每個輸出作為後續輸入，以在低FPR下改善成員證據。我們展示了記憶的訓練樣本在迭代再生過程中表現出顯著更高的連貫性和較慢的退化速度，與非成員生成相比。我們的結果顯示，MADreMIA在多種模型家族和模態中提供了更豐富的信號；我們呈現了對IAR、擴散和語言模型的全面評估，以及顯示其對音頻模型潛力的初步結果。
2606.31991.author: Wojciech Łapacz et.al.
